g.text: 0x20 | timer_entry_t*| timer_pool // ptr → heap timer slots
0x28 | int* | timer_heap // ptr → heap index array
0x40 | int | deferred_head // ring buf read idx
0x44 | int | deferred_tail // ring buf write idx
0x48 | int | pending_count // deferred epoll ops count
Total: 32 bytes = 0.5 cache lines — 2 handlers per cache line
0x04 | int | timer_id // opaque ID returned to caller
0x08 | uint32_t | events // epoll flags for ADD/MOD
HOT FIELDS — touched every epoll_wait() tick
0x00 | int (4B) | epoll_fd // syscall arg every tick
0x08 | int (4B) | dispatching // set=1 before loop, 0 after
All 4 fields fit in 16 bytes → same cache line as each other
0x10 | ptr (8B) | handlers // load on every fd dispatch
0x18 | int (4B) | max_fds // bounds check on accept
0x20 | ptr (8B) | timer_pool // load during timer expiry
0x28 | ptr (8B) | timer_heap // load during sift ops
0x30 | int (4B) | timer_pool_size // bounds check on insert
0x38 | int (4B) | next_timer_id // increment on timer create
Spans cache lines 0–1; timer ptrs warm only during timer phase
0x48 | int (4B) | pending_count // pending epoll ops count
deferred[] and pending_ops[] are cold until Phase 2/3 of tick
Phase 4: Expire timers (heap root check loop)
epoll_fd | Phase 1 | syscall arg — must be in reg/L1
running | Phase 1 | while() check — branch predictor
dispatching | Ph 1–2 | set/clear — write → L1 invalidate
handlers[fd] | Phase 1 | pointer chase — L3 miss risk
timer_heap[0] | Phase 4 | root of min-heap — always L1 hot
deferred[] | Phase 3 | cold unless callbacks deferred
pending_ops[] | Phase 2 | cold unless callbacks modified epoll
